root_cause: Container entrypoint exists but cannot be executed due to permission denial
evidence: Kubelet or runtime reports exec-style permission denied while starting the container
Container remains in a waiting startup error state

Dominant exec error: Error: failed to start container "api": Error response from daemon: OCI runtime create failed: runc create failed: unable to start container process: exec: "/app/start.sh": permission denied: unknown

causes.message: Container runtime attempted to execute the configured entrypoint or startup command
Entrypoint or startup script exists but is not executable by the container runtime
Container remains stuck before successful startup

pod:orders-api-7fd6b7b8cf-jg2sm: Pod cannot start because the configured entrypoint is not executable

likely_causes: Entrypoint script or binary lacks execute permission in the image
Image build copied the startup script without preserving executable mode
Container runs as a user that cannot execute the configured entrypoint
suggested_checks: Inspect the image entrypoint or command and file permissions
Verify the startup script is executable in the built image
Check the container user and filesystem ownership of the entrypoint
